metadata.title: Validate Your Build Problem Formulation Before Running Codex
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: If you can't describe what the build does in one sentence, you haven't decided yet — and Codex will decide for you.

The one-sentence formulation test has three questions: What does this build do? What does it touch — read-only vs. write access to specific paths and APIs? What does it never touch — explicit out-of-scope items? If any answer requires more than one sentence, the formulation isn't done.

claude "Apply the three-question formulation test to this build description. Answer each in exactly one sentence. If any answer requires more than one sentence, flag that question as 'formulation not complete'." < build_description.txt

props.command: claude "Apply the three-question formulation test: (1) What does this build do? (2) What does it touch? (3) What does it never touch? Flag any answer that needs more than one sentence." < build_description.txt

beats.narration_text: formulation_validator.py — applies three-question test, validates each answer for one-sentence constraint, produces traffic light per question: green for pass, red for fail with annotation.

props.code:
# applies three-question formulation test and produces traffic-light result
import formulation_engine

desc = open('build_description.txt').read()
result = formulation_engine.test(desc)
for q, answer, passed in result:
    status = 'GREEN' if passed else 'RED'
    print(f'Q{q}: [{status}] {answer[:60]}...')
    if not passed:
        print(f'  Missing: {formulation_engine.missing(q, answer)}')

beats.narration_text: Three question cards animate in. Q1 red — vague: build a feedback notifier needs more than one sentence. Q2 green — specific: reads JSON from playtests folder, writes one dot-md to playtests. Q3 green — never writes to src or modifies game code. Below Q1's red light: annotation — Missing: what trigger, what input format, what output location.
Fix the Q1 formulation: give a precise one-sentence description.

props.command: claude "Rewrite: 'A script that reads JSON feedback exports from beta testers, filters to current build version, groups by game system, and writes one markdown summary to playtests/v{build}.md.'" < q1_vague.txt

beats.narration_text: Same three cards from B04, but Q1 answer now reads: 'A script that reads JSON feedback exports from beta testers, filters to current build version, groups by game system, and writes one markdown summary to playtests/v-build.md.' Q1 traffic light turns from red to green. FORMULATION READY appears below all three cards.

The one-sentence constraint is a forcing function: it requires you to have decided. 'Build a feedback notifier' is a category. 'A script that reads JSON feedback exports, filters to current build version, groups by system, and writes one markdown summary to playtests/v-build.md' is a decision — it names the input, the filter, the grouping, and the output.
Next: write a handoff condition that catches what tests miss.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist plus Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
